caption: A woman wearing a green short-sleeved top with light-colored trim stands in a dusty, open dirt area in front of low-slung buildings. In the background, several people are scattered across the grounds, including individuals in casual attire and a person in a blue headwrap and patterned skirt. Two white vehicles are parked in the distance near flags flying from poles. The woman holds a small piece of white paper in her hand. As the sequence progresses, the camera shifts from showing her back and side profile to a front-facing shot where she speaks toward the camera. On-screen text appears across the bottom of the scenes, stating "When I first found out that Nelson Mandela was dead," followed by "four days had already passed." and concluding with "I find myself to be abandoned, as if in a grave, but I am not buried."
